description: Queen Patch 11 / Season 9 Patch (5 May 2020) — Mobile Improvements, Outlands Banks, Season 9 Changes (Territory Points, World Boss Points, Crystal League, Alliance Penalties, Rank Thresholds), Increased Enemy Rewards, World Map Improvements, UI Improvements, Terrain, Audio, Crafting, Durability Loss Changes, Combat Balance Changes, Fixes.

sections.heading: Mobile Improvements
sections.description: First version of an all-new way to play Albion on mobile (Android and iOS). Featuring virtual joystick controls, updated HUD layout, all-new scaling for menus, interface zoom camera, and gesture improvements.
items: Virtual joystick for movement that snaps to the initial press position
Auto-targeting of attacks and spells: single press targets the closest enemy (favoring look direction); long press tries to target self
Manual drag-targeting of ground-target spells and skillshots by dragging their respective buttons (cast on release; cancel by releasing on area marked with X)
Context-based interactions for talking, entering, gathering, fishing, and collecting

Updated HUD layout: top-right minimap with reworked cluster label (zone player count and time moved to new row; zone color indicator centered; label can be pressed to open cluster map or long-pressed to hide)
Compact Party and Quest view on left side of HUD; compact retractable queues; new compact Destiny Board Tracker
Inventory button shows current load as colored circle-fill; larger player HUD elements; new button and icon graphics
Additional glow effect and target arrow to currently selected targets
Default in-game zoom level increased to 40%
New Options page: switch between joystick and tap/mouse controls; Tap to Move option; Dynamic Actions option; Spell Drag-Targeting option
Tap Controls: new layouts; simple drag-targeting (drag spell into world to aim, release to cast, drag back to cancel)
Additional scaling on almost all interface windows based on device size
New zoom option for interfaces with pinch-zoom and zoom steps; double tap to open/close
Improved list scrolling, item equipping, and looting via drag and double tap
Long-press gestures: holding certain buttons longer triggers different interactions
sections.searchable_text: Mobile Improvements virtual joystick movement auto-targeting spells drag-targeting HUD layout minimap cluster label Party Quest Destiny Board Tracker Inventory zoom tap controls Android iOS gestures long press scroll

sections.heading: Outlands Banks
sections.description: Personal bank space in the Outlands, known as Outlands Banks, can now be unlocked at numerous locations.
items: Any given Outlands Bank location can be unlocked with a one-time Silver payment
Once a specific bank is unlocked, it can be used forever
Any number of locations can be unlocked by paying the Silver fee for each
Storage is local and does not transfer between bank locations
These banks are located in PvP-enabled zones — guards will not interfere if you are attacked or attack others
sections.searchable_text: Outlands Banks personal bank Silver payment forever local storage PvP zones guards

sections.heading: Season 9 Change: Territory Season Point and Siphoned Energy Storage
sections.description: Season Points and Siphoned Energy generated by farm and resource territories are now split into two parts, in a ratio based on the zone's proximity to the center of the Outlands.
items: One part is stored locally in the territory; the other is transferred directly once per day to the guild currently holding the territory
Accumulated points and energy are paid out on three specific dates to whichever guild holds the territory at that point (Season 9 dates: June 13, July 11, August 8)
If the territory is conquered, the new owner immediately seizes 20% of all stored energy and another 20% is destroyed
Giving up the territory (e.g. to transfer to an ally) also destroys 20% of stored points and energy
The specific percent of Season Points and Energy stored varies based on the zone's proximity to the center of the Outlands
sections.searchable_text: Season 9 Territory Season Points Siphoned Energy Storage split ratio Outlands center guild conquered 20% destroyed transfer ally June July August

sections.heading: Season 9 Change: Season Points from Veteran and World Bosses
sections.description: Veteran Bosses (found in static open-world dungeons) and World Bosses in the Outlands now give Season Points.
items: Tier 5: Veteran Boss — 3 points
Tier 6: Veteran Boss — 4 points
Tier 7: Veteran Boss — 4 points / World Boss — 40 points
Tier 8: Veteran Boss — 5 points / World Boss — 50 points
More Season Points are earned the closer you are to the center of the Outlands, with a maximum of up to 6x the base amount
sections.searchable_text: Season 9 Season Points Veteran Bosses World Bosses static dungeons Outlands T5 T6 T7 T8 3 4 5 40 50 points center 6x multiplier

sections.heading: Season 9 Change: Crystal League Adjustments
items: Level 1 Crystal League matches are no longer full-loot — players killed in Level 1 respawn with all gear intact (as in Arena); Levels 2–9 remain full-loot
Level 1 Token: buyable from Energy Manipulator for 100,000 Silver
Level 2 Token: buyable for 200,000 Silver
Level 3 Token: buyable for 400,000 Silver

Maximum rewards adjusted: Level 1: 200,000 Silver / 1 Season Point | Level 2: 400,000 / 5 SP | Level 3: 800,000 / 25 SP | Level 4: 1,000,000 / 75 SP | Level 5: 1,300,000 / 200 SP | Level 6: 1,750,000 / 500 SP | Level 7: 2,500,000 / 1000 SP | Level 8: 3,500,000 / 2500 SP | Level 9: 5,000,000 / 5000 SP | Off-Season Level 3: 1,100,000 Silver
Matches of Level 4 and above now happen much more frequently
Level 1 and 2 Item Power Thresholds have been lowered
The break between Level 4, 5, 6, and 7 matches reduced from 60 minutes to 30 minutes
Enabled scrim matches for the Crystal Realm map: /cm (playername) crystal
sections.searchable_text: Season 9 Crystal League Level 1 full-loot Arena Energy Manipulator Silver tokens 100000 200000 400000 rewards Season Points Level 4 5 6 7 break 60 minutes 30 minutes Item Power Thresholds scrim

sections.heading: Season 9 Change: Alliance Penalty Adjustments
sections.searchable_text: Season 9 Alliance Penalty Siphoned Energy Drain territories 10 3% per increment Fame Silver Penalty 20 territories 1% per territory

subsections.heading: Siphoned Energy Drain
subsections.searchable_text: Siphoned Energy Drain 10 territories 3% per increment penalty reduction total

changes.ability: Siphoned Energy Drain — increment per territory:
changes.raw_text: Penalty still starts at 10 territories but now increases in increments of 3% per additional territory (was 5%). Example: 10 Territories = 3% less; 11 Territories = 6% less; 12 Territories = 9% less, etc.
notes: Example: 10 territories = 3% less total Siphoned Energy; 11 = 6%; 12 = 9%; etc.

subsections.heading: Fame and Silver Penalty
subsections.searchable_text: Fame Silver Penalty 10 territories 20 territories 1% per territory open-world PvE activities

changes.ability: Fame and Silver Penalty — starting threshold:
changes.raw_text: The penalty now starts at 20 territories (was 10) and increases by 1% per additional territory. Applies to Silver and Fame earned from open-world PvE and activities.
notes: Penalty still increases by 1% per additional territory beyond the threshold
Applies to Silver and Fame earned from open-world PvE and activities

sections.heading: Season 9 Change: Increased Rank Point Thresholds
sections.description: Made to reflect the higher pool of Season Points obtainable in Season 9.
sections.searchable_text: Rank Point Thresholds Crystal Gold Silver Bronze Season 9 240000 75000 20000 5000

sections.heading: Increased Fame, Silver, and Respawn Time for High-Level Enemies
sections.description: Rewards for killing Veteran and Elite Enemies buffed at World Boss Locations and Static dungeons.
sections.searchable_text: Fame Silver Respawn Time Veteran Elite Enemies World Boss static dungeons 30% respawn window 15 25 30 minutes 20 22.5 average

subsections.heading: High-Level Enemy Rewards
subsections.searchable_text: Fame 30% Silver loot drops 30% respawn window 15 25 minutes 15 30 minutes average 20 22.5

changes.ability: Veteran / Elite Enemy Rewards (World Boss Locations and Static Dungeons):
changes.raw_text: Fame rewarded: +30%; Overall Silver and Silver-related loot drops: +30%; Respawn window: 15–25 minutes → 15–30 minutes (average 20 → 22.5 minutes)
notes: Overall Silver and Silver-related loot drops increased by 30%

sections.heading: World Map and Minimap Improvements
items: Reworked and improved overall visuals of world map
World map political view: Alliance view now also shows zones owned by guilds without an alliance
Added a "Prime Time" view to map viewing options, allowing players to see all zones in a given prime time
Smart Cluster Queue: minimap display for players queueing for an overcrowded entrance reworked to differentiate it from regular zerg indicators
Adjusted road minimap colors in steppe zones for better readability
sections.searchable_text: World Map Minimap Improvements political view Alliance guilds Prime Time Smart Cluster Queue zerg indicators steppe zones road colors readability

sections.heading: UI Improvements: Updated Mail System
sections.description: The Mail Interface has been updated to the current interface style.
items: Personal inbox can now be filtered by various categories for easier management
Personal and guild inboxes now support pagination, allowing players to read beyond their last 50 received messages
Marketplace mails now include a countdown timer, after which they are automatically deleted
Messages can now be set to unread
sections.searchable_text: UI Mail System inbox filter categories pagination 50 messages Marketplace countdown timer unread

sections.heading: UI Improvements: Updated Building Interface
sections.description: The Building Interface has been updated to the current interface style.
items: Economy, Farming, and Military buildings now show which products can be crafted or raised there
Buildings are now represented on the cluster map by the same icon used in the Building UI and on the actual building model
sections.searchable_text: UI Building Interface Economy Farming Military crafted raised cluster map icon

sections.heading: UI Improvements: Carry Capacity Display
items: Max Load Capacity now refers to 100% of player capacity (previously referred to 200%, which was confusing)
Carry weight bonuses displayed in item details are now halved because of this change
From a gameplay perspective nothing has changed — only the way it is displayed
Load status display in character details now more clearly shows when movement speed will be impaired
sections.searchable_text: UI Carry Capacity Display Max Load 100% 200% carry weight bonuses halved movement speed impaired character details

sections.heading: UI Improvements: Display Order of Active Armor Spells
sections.description: Active spells for regular T4+ armor are now displayed in the armor UI in a consistent order from left to right: first — spell shared between all armors of the same slot; second — spell shared between all armors of the same slot and category; third — spell unique to that armor only.
items: First: spell shared between all armors of the same slot (e.g. "Mend Wounds" for all Chest Armors)
Second: spell shared between all armors of the same slot and category (e.g. "Taunt" for all Plate Chest Armors)
Third: spell unique to that armor only (e.g. "Fury" for Soldier Armor)
sections.searchable_text: UI Active Armor Spells display order T4 slot category unique Mend Wounds Taunt Fury Soldier Armor Plate Chest

sections.heading: Additional UI Improvements
items: Kill Details UI (accessible via personal stats menu) updated to current interface style; now shows all equipped gear for killer and victim
Added search fields to guild member and friend lists
Added an "inspect" popup for incoming duel invites to allow inspection of challengers' equipped items
Added new options "Support" and "Battle Mount" to party role selection; made "Auto" the default role for new players
Improved Gold Market layout and added summary fields to ordering screen
Islands designated as favorites are now represented with a star in the travel destinations list
Direwolf icon has been updated

New icons created for: Dagger E (Bloodthirsty Blade), Dagger Pair E (Slit Throat), Heavy Crossbow E (Sweeping Bolt), Clarent Blade E (Mighty Swing), Weeping Repeater E (Explosive Mine), Frost Staffs Q (Hoarfrost), Frost Staffs Q (Ice Shard), Hoarfrost Staff E (Avalanche), Infernal Scythe E (Bloody Reap), Bows Q (Poisoned Arrow), Bows W (Ray of Light), Warbow E (Magic Arrow), Blight Staff E (Ruthless Nature), Heron Spear E (Spear Throw)
sections.searchable_text: UI Kill Details equipped gear guild member friend list search inspect duel invite Support Battle Mount Auto party role Gold Market favorites star Direwolf icons Bloodthirsty Blade Slit Throat Sweeping Bolt Mighty Swing Explosive Mine Hoarfrost Ice Shard Avalanche Bloody Reap Poisoned Arrow Ray of Light Magic Arrow Ruthless Nature Spear Throw

sections.heading: Terrain Visibility Improvements
sections.description: Certain terrain elements in Mountain biome zones that could potentially block player views have been reworked.
items: Players in affected Mountain biome zones when the patch is deployed will be migrated to the nearest exit upon login
sections.searchable_text: Terrain Visibility Improvements Mountain biome zones player views migration exit

sections.heading: Audio Improvements
items: Added new crop farming sounds
Polished numerous mount sounds and fixed an issue with doubled footsteps for hoofed mounts
Polished all hide mob sounds according to their size and type
sections.searchable_text: Audio Improvements crop farming sounds mount hoofed footsteps hide mob sounds

sections.heading: Crafting and Studying Changes
items: Decreased crafting time of potions and adjusted their weights
Corrected T7 Gigantify potions to require 72 Firetouched Mullein instead of 71
Corrected T8 Poison potion to require 36 Firetouched Mullein and Dragon Teasel instead of 24
Overhauled crafting and refining times across the game — significant reductions in most cases (resources, armor, weapons, tools, furniture, mounts, intermediary farming products)
Further reduced building time based on tier of resources (T8 lowered by ~25%, T1/T2 by ~90%)
Non-refundable items such as artifacts are no longer included in the Focus cost of crafting
Siege Ballista, Flame Basilisks, and Venom Basilisks can no longer be crafted with Focus
Changed crafting ingredient requirements of Avalonian Sandwiches from milk to butter; slightly adjusted crafting times
Increased weight of Fancy Fish Sauce to 1.5kg and Special Fish Sauce to 2kg
Reduced crafting time of fish sauce from 1 second to 0.02 seconds
Increased chopped fish requirement of Special Fish Sauce from 125 to 135
Studying will now calculate its Fame bonus from Focus based on the local production bonus rather than resource return rate
sections.searchable_text: Crafting Studying potions weights T7 Gigantify Firetouched Mullein T8 Poison Dragon Teasel refining times armor weapons tools furniture mounts building time T8 T1 T2 Focus artifacts Siege Ballista Flame Basilisk Venom Basilisk Avalonian Sandwiches milk butter Fancy Fish Sauce Special Fish Sauce chopped fish 125 135 production bonus

sections.heading: Increased Durability Loss Based on Number of Attackers
sections.description: Item durability loss now scales based on the number of players who attacked the victim. Only applies in areas where PvP combat is lethal. Does not apply when all players are faction flagged, during Crystal League Battles, or in Hellgates.
items: 1 attacker: 20–40% durability loss per item
2 attackers: 23–46%
3 attackers: 26–52%
4 attackers: 29–58%
5 attackers: 32–64%
6 attackers: 35–70%
7 attackers: 38–76%
8 attackers: 41–82%
9 attackers: 44–88%
10 attackers: 47–94%
11+ attackers: 50–100%
Durability loss upon death after knockdown (e.g. by a mob) has been increased to the same amount as being killed directly by another player
sections.searchable_text: Durability Loss attackers scaling PvP lethal faction Crystal League Hellgates knockdown mob 20% 100%

sections.heading: Other Changes
sections.searchable_text: Other Changes Faction Flagging Hideout fast travel chest guards Magic Pollen invisibility Portal Zones protective bubble Darkbough Snag Sunfang Wasteland passage stone Focus Solo Randomized Dungeon despawn respawn Blue Zone Silver bags rankings

subsections.heading: Other Changes
subsections.searchable_text: Fast travel Hideout Faction Flagging chest guards Magic Pollen Portal Zones protective bubble Darkbough Snag Sunfang Wasteland stone Focus Solo Randomized Dungeon 60s 30s 300s portal Silver bags rankings

changes.ability: Solo Randomized Dungeon portal despawn countdown:
changes.raw_text: Time until a Solo Randomized Dungeon portal begins its despawn countdown after being cleared: 60 seconds → 30 seconds

changes.ability: Solo Randomized Dungeon portal respawn in Blue Zone:
changes.raw_text: Time until a Solo Randomized Dungeon portal respawns in a Blue Zone after a portal has been removed: 300 seconds → 60 seconds
notes: Fast-traveling to a Hideout now removes Faction Flagging
Attacking chests near enemies now alerts the enemies as intended
The Invisibility from spell "Magic Pollen" will now break on cast — no longer possible to use it to avoid chest guards
Exits from Outlands Portal Zones now have "safe" areas in the adjacent zone that grant a protective bubble
The passage between Darkbough Snag and Sunfang Wasteland was removed to reduce power projection at the center of the Outlands
Refining stone blocks with Focus now requires a consistent amount of Focus across all enchantment levels of stone
Silver bags no longer count toward "Silver Collected" player rankings

sections.searchable_text: Combat Balance Changes Arcane Staffs Void Malevolent Locus Cooldown Item Power Mounts Tower Chariot Tower Shield Colossus Beetle Base Move Speed Flight

subsections.heading: Arcane Staffs
subsections.searchable_text: Arcane Staffs Void Malevolent Locus Cooldown 900 IP 1200 IP 1700 IP 48.6 46 45 40 39 30

changes.raw_text: Void (Malevolent Locus): Reduced Cooldowns based on Item Power: 900 IP: 48.6 → 46; 1200 IP: 45 → 40; 1700 IP: 39 → 30

subsections.heading: Mounts
subsections.searchable_text: Mounts Tower Chariot Tower Shield Cast Time Cooldown Colossus Beetle Base Move Speed Flight Speed Bonus

changes.raw_text: Tower Shield (Tower Chariot): Cast Time: 1s → 0.6s; Cooldown: 60s → 30s
Colossus Beetle: Base Move Speed: 130% → 110%. Flight: Cast Time: 0.5s → 0.8s; Increases Speed Bonus by 20% (to make up for the reduction in base speed)
notes: Flight now increases Speed Bonus by 20% to compensate for the reduction in base move speed

sections.heading: Fixes
items: Fixed an issue where player appearance would sometimes glitch after killing another player or changing items
Enemy player outlines have been changed from green to red as intended
Chests in Hellgates now reset their HP when players leave the area, as other enemies and chests do
Dungeons and passages now yield fish that corresponds to their tier — the Underway changed from T8 to T7 accordingly
Fixed a case where "you will lose progress if you pick up" prompt was shown for farmables whether you had progress or not
Fixed a Watchtower UI issue where the incorrect level would display for unclaimed towers and players without guilds
Fixed a bug that caused "Completed" tab to disappear when "Take All" is clicked and there are filtered completed auctions left to show
Fixed an issue where some True Damage abilities scaled with some damage buffs — now they remain constant as intended
Fixed an issue where over-time effects would sometimes interrupt uninterruptible spells
Fixed an issue where chests in the Exalted Undercrypt were not protected by enemies
Resolved a case that could prevent the Destiny Board bonus from applying to the yield when fishing
Numerous additional graphical, terrain, animation, UI, audio, and localization fixes
sections.searchable_text: Fixes player appearance glitch enemy outlines green red Hellgates chests HP Underway T8 T7 farmables progress Watchtower unclaimed guilds Completed tab auctions True Damage over-time uninterruptible Exalted Undercrypt Destiny Board fishing yield graphical terrain animation UI audio localization
